metadata.definition: A technology milestone is a means or procedure developed by humans to transform, control, or organize matter, energy, or information, which (1) grants capabilities previously impossible, (2) is widely adopted, (3) enables subsequent technological development.
academic_references: Singer, C. (1954-1984) A History of Technology, 8 vols, Oxford UP
Needham, J. (1954-2008) Science and Civilisation in China, 27 vols, Cambridge UP
Forbes, R.J. (1955-1964) Studies in Ancient Technology, 9 vols, Brill
Mokyr, J. (1990) The Lever of Riches, Oxford UP
Basalla, G. (1988) The Evolution of Technology, Cambridge UP
Arthur, W.B. (2009) The Nature of Technology, Free Press
Smil, V. (2005) Creating the Twentieth Century, Oxford UP
Bresnahan, T. & Trajtenberg, M. (1995) General Purpose Technologies, J. Econometrics
Perez, C. (2002) Technological Revolutions and Financial Capital, Edward Elgar
Seshat: Global History Databank (2011-present), Evolution Institute

provenance.generation_method: LLM-generated (Claude Opus 4.6), not extracted from primary sources
provenance.external_validation: Schema design informed by Singer, Mokyr, Seshat, Arthur, Bresnahan & Trajtenberg. Individual entries NOT cross-validated against primary sources.
known_limitations: All entries are LLM-generated and may contain historical inaccuracies
Geographic bias: predominantly Western-centric for post-1760 entries
Year precision varies: ancient entries may have ±500 year uncertainty
Target quantity (3,380) was LLM-estimated, not derived from external benchmark
Recommended: cross-validate a random sample against Encyclopedia Britannica or Seshat before publication
provenance.recommended_citation: LLM-assisted technology milestone compilation. Use with caution for quantitative claims.
